headline: AI Models Split on SPY Direction Amid Iran Tensions and GM Earnings Beat
summary: Models showed stark disagreement on market direction, with Claude bearish on SPY citing Iran conflict and oil surge above $100, while other models remained bullish on tech momentum. GM emerged as the highest conviction call at 85% confidence after beating Q1 earnings with $3.70 EPS vs $2.62 expected.

consensus_picks.note: Claude and GPT-4o predicted down citing geopolitical tensions, while Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity saw upside.
Three models agreed NVDA would reach $220 on continued semiconductor demand and AI enthusiasm.
Tech-heavy index saw bullish consensus with targets around $450-670 based on strong 5-day momentum.
Both models cited production concerns and EV competition pressuring Tesla lower.

best_call.summary: Claude's 85% confidence GM call backed by specific earnings beat data ($3.70 vs $2.62 EPS) and raised guidance stands out as most conviction.

worst_call.summary: Results pending - market close needed to determine worst performer.
